notes: Vakataka. The Vakataka dynasty: Founded by Vindhyashakti around 250-270 CE. The Vakatakas ruled over Central and Western India.

Maitrakas established Valabhi as their capital and ruled until the middle of the eighth century. Dhruvasena II was a contemporary of Harshavardhana and was married to his daughter. Hsuan Tsang tells us that Dhruvasena II attended Harsha’s assembly at Prayaga (Allahabad). Maukharies ruled over Kannauj, a city in western Uttar Pradesh, they were also the subordinate rulers of the Guptas and used the title of samanta.

Harisena. He was a Sanskrit writer and poet in the court of Samudragupta. "Brihat Katha Kosh" is a collection of ancient Indian folk tales and legends. Bhavabhuti is believed to be the court poet of King Yashovarman of Kannauj. His famous plays are Mahaviracharita, Malatimadhava.

Chandragupta II, also known as Vikramaditya, ruled from 380 to 415 CE and was a renowned patron of arts and literature. Kalidasa's notable works, such as "Abhijnanasakuntalam", "Meghaduta", and "Raghuvamsa", showcase his mastery of Sanskrit literature.

Samudragupta. He was the son and successor of Chandragupta I and was the greatest ruler of the Gupta dynasty. The Allahabad Pillar inscription, also called Prayag Prashasti, was composed by Harishena.

Maharajadhiraja. Chandragupta I, the son of Gupta king Ghatotkacha and grandson of the dynasty's founder Sri Gupta, is referred to as Maharaja in the Allahabad Pillar inscription, while his successor, Kumaragupta, adopted the title "Mahendraditya."

Chandragupta II, also known History as Vikramaditya, was the third ruler of the Gupta Empire. He is often identified with King Chandra from the Delhi iron pillar inscription. Notable Travellers and Their Visits - Abdul Razzak (Iran): Deva Raya II. Francois Bernier (France): Shah Jahan, Ibn Batuta (Morocco): Mohammed Bin Tughlaq, Megasthenes (Greece): Chandragupta Maurya, Thomas Roe and William Hawkins (Britain): Jahangir, Hwien Tsang (China): Harsha Vardhana.

Gupta. Bagh Caves are located in Gwalior (Madhya Pradesh). These are a group of nine rock-cut monuments. These caves were founded by the Buddhist monk Dataka. Caves in India - Undavalli caves (Andhra Pradesh), Barabar Caves (Bihar), Badami Cave Temples (Karnataka), Udaygiri Caves (Madhya Pradesh), Ajanta Caves and Ellora Caves (Maharashtra). Q.130. The Vakataka dynasty was directly related to which Gupta emperor? SSC CGL 20/07/2023 (4th shift) (a) Chandra Gupta I (b) Shree Gupta (c) Samudra Gupta (d) Chandra Gupta II Sol.130.(d) Chandra Gupta II. Vakataka dynasty : Founded by Vindhyashakti in 250 CE. Ajanta caves (numbers ⅩⅥ, ⅩⅦ, ⅩⅨ) are the best examples of Vakataka excellence in the field of painting. Q.131. In which period is the first inscriptional example of the practice of Sati found? SSC CGL 26/07/2023 (4th shift) (a) Maukhari (b) Vardhana (c) Satavahana (d) Gupta Sol.131.(d) Gupta. Practice of Sati has been found in the Eran Pillar Inscription of Bhanugupta, Madhya Pradesh. Sati Movement : Raja Ram Mohan Roy described ‘Sati’ as ‘murder’ according to every shastra. By his efforts, Bengal Sati Regulation Act was passed to criminalize the practice of Sati in 1829.

Allahabad pillar inscription composed in Sanskrit by Harishena, the court poet of Samudragupta. Q.133. Who was the court poet of Harshavardhana? SSC MTS 03/05/2023 (Afternoon) (a) Hema Saraswati (b) Parmanand (c) Banabhatta (d) Sri Ponna Sol.133.(c) Banabhatta - His teacher's name was Bhatsu or Bharvu. Banabhatta's three works are famous - “Harshacharita”, “Kadambari” and “Chandishtak”. Harshvardhan was the ruler of the Pushyabhuti dynasty of Thaneshwar. Q.134. Who among the following Navratnas from Chandragupta II court wrote 'Mantrashastra’ ? SSC MTS 12/05/2023 (Morning) (a) Kalidasa (b) Ghatakarapara (c) Vetala Bhatta (d) Amarasimha Sol.134.(c) Vetala Bhatta. Nine jewels (Navratna) of Chandragupta II - Amarasimha, Dhanvantari, Harisena, Kalidasa, Kshapanaka, Shanku, Varahamihira, Varuchi, Vetala-Bhatta.
Chandragupta II (successor of Samudragupta). Kumaragupta I : He was also known as Shakraditya and Mahendraditya. He founded Nalanda Mahavihara in 5th century CE. His successor - Skandagupta.

Samudragupta - He was a ruler of the Gupta dynasty. On several of his coins, he was depicted as seated on a sofa and playing a Veena. He is known as the Napoleon of India. He was given the title of Kaviraj.

Chandragupta II - He was the son of Samudragupta and Datta Devi. He ruled the Gupta Empire from 380 to 415 AD during the Golden Age of India. He established the Mehrauli iron pillar. Q.138. Who among the following succeeded Ghatotkacha, the Gupta ruler? SSC MTS 20/06/2023 (Evening) (a) Kumaragupta I (b) Chandragupta I (c) Chandragupta II (d) Skandagupta Sol.138.(b) Chandragupta I. Founder - Sri Gupta, Next Successors - Ghatotkacha, Chandragupta I, Samudragupta, Chandragupta II, Kumaragupta I (founded Nalanda University).

Gupta dynasty. In Mehrauli (Delhi) an iron pillar was constructed by the famous king Chandragupta II, India, notable for the rust-resistant composition of the metal used in its construction. The inscription on the pillar is in Sanskrit, about Gupta emperor Chandragupta II.

Nagara-shreshthi (Chief banker or merchant). Offices held by Poet Harishena: Maha-danda-Nayaka (Chief Judicial Officer), Kumar-amatya (Important minister) and a sandhi - vigrahika (Minister of War and Peace). Q.141. Fa Xian began his journey back home from which of the following Indian state? SSC CGL 08/12/2022 (1st Shift) (a) Bihar (b) Maharashtra (c) Bengal (d) Odisha Sol.141.(c) Bengal. Fa-Xian (Chinese traveller) started his journey back home from Bengal by boarding a merchant ship. He traveled from China to India on foot to acquire Buddhist texts. He came to India, under the reign of Chandragupta II, and arrived at Pataliputra from the northwest.

Licchavi. Samudragupta (335 -375 CE, Kaviraja) was the son of Chandragupta I and Queen Kumaradevi (Licchavi clan). He is called ‘Napoleon of India’ by V.A. Smith. Q.143. In the context of Gupta administration, the term 'vithi' referred to a/an: SSC MTS 05/10/2021 (Evening) (a) elephantry unit (b) administrative unit (c) personal guard of the king (d) soldier Sol.143.(b) Administrative unit. In Gupta's period, there was a hierarchy of administrative divisions from top to bottom. The empire was called by various names such as Rajya, Rashtra, Desha, Mandala, Prithvi, and Avani. It was divided into provinces, which were further divided into Vishayas. A Vishayapati administered the Vishaya. A part of the Vishaya was called Vithi.
Chandragupta-I. The Lichchhavi clan was headquartered at Vaishali in present-day Bihar during the time of Gautama Buddha.
